original_hypothesis.title: EpochController initialization/deployment gaps can leave performUpkeep paths unusable or abusable by mis-set registries
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: EpochController.initialize does not set genesisManager, and performUpkeep/performPreUpkeep depend on automationRegistry/automationRegistry2 and genesisManager being correctly configured. If owner misconfigures registries or forgets to set genesisManager, epoch flips revert and emissions/fees distribution halt. If owner sets automationRegistry to an unintended address, that address can call upkeep functions (subject to checkUpkeep conditions) potentially at undesired times, increasing operational fragility.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Although owner-controlled, this is a recurring real-world failure mode: missing wiring causes systemic halts. Scripts show registries being set post-deploy, but no script evidence shows genesisManager setup before epoch flips.
deployment_assumptions: genesisManager is not set before the first upkeep calls
automationRegistry/automationRegistry2 can be set incorrectly or left unset in production
EPOCH_MANAGER role may be broader than intended in PermissionsRegistry
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Provided scripts set automationRegistry and automationRegistry2 (deployment-flows/scripts/contract-deployments/set-chainlink.js) but no provided script sets EpochController.setGenesisManager before calling performUpkeep (deployment-flows/scripts/flip-epoch.js).

proof_of_signal.pattern: initialize does not set genesisManager; performUpkeep calls genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip() without nonzero check

preconditions: Upkeep is triggered (automationRegistry or EPOCH_MANAGER role caller) when IMinter.check() returns true

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Critical epoch flip depends on post-deploy wiring not enforced on-chain
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Owner configuration required; EPOCH_MANAGER role expands execution set

operational_questions: Is there a guaranteed deployment checklist ensuring genesisManager is set before enabling automation?
Who holds EPOCH_MANAGER role and how is it managed?
Are automationRegistry addresses correct per-network and immutable in ops processes?
validation_requirements: Review deployment runbooks and executed transactions
Simulate performUpkeep with genesisManager unset and observe failure modes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait for epoch flip condition (IMinter.check() true)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Call performUpkeep from authorized caller while genesisManager is unset to cause revert and operational incident
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Repeat to keep system in failed state until corrected (griefing if EPOCH_MANAGER role is broad)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Epoch flip failures block emissions and fee distribution
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium (requires privileged configuration change and potential catch-up distribution)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All weekly/epoch emissions and fee distribution processes

execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: Low to Medium (depends on access to authorized caller role)
execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: At epoch boundary when check() is true

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Reverts in performUpkeep due to zero genesisManager; registry changes
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Missed epoch flips; lack of GaugeManager distributions after expected time
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Repeated upkeep attempts from EPOCH_MANAGER addresses

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
